prompt: Two-commit refactor branch refactor/consolidate-retry. Repo has .github/pull_request_template.md with five headings (Description, Type of change, Testing, Reviewer notes, Checklist). Draft a PR title + body.

assertions.text: Title uses plain imperative style (matching repo history, which is not Conventional Commits)
Body uses the template's exact five headings in order: Description, Type of change, Testing, Reviewer notes, Checklist
Description section emphasizes that behavior is unchanged (the refactor's defining property)
Type of change has the 'Refactor (no behavior change)' box ticked, and ONLY that one
Reviewer notes guide a reviewer to src/retry.py first (the new helper), then the call-site migration in src/client.py
Checklist boxes are ticked honestly — no false claims (e.g. 'tests added' should NOT be ticked since none were)
HTML comments from the template (<!-- ... -->) have been removed or addressed
Body does NOT enumerate the two commits (no 'Commits:' section)
Body does NOT contain tooling references ('Claude', 'AI', 'verified by', test-output dumps)
Body does not invent top-level headings not in the template (e.g. 'Risks', 'Why now') — those go inside Description if needed
